name: North Carolina Licensing Board for General Contractors Verify License Search

agency.name: North Carolina Licensing Board for General Contractors

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the official search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: North Carolina public utilities contractor licenses
North Carolina specialty contractor licenses and qualifiers exposed by the public search
knownExclusions: This source is scoped to the North Carolina general contractors board and does not cover every construction-related license or local authorization in North Carolina.
Electrical, plumbing, heating, fire sprinkler, and refrigeration contractors are tracked through separate North Carolina board source entries.
Scope depends on the license classification, limitation, and qualifier shown by the official source.
The official source is lookup-oriented; bulk file availability has not been confirmed.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Any future access should respect lookup controls and prefer official bulk options if available.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed in this phase.
researchNotes: Useful source for general-contractor verification semantics, including building, residential, highway, public utilities, and specialty classifications. Official solar-panel guidance says SPVP installations are electrical work unless structural or other general-contractor work is included. This source is not broad enough to represent all North Carolina skilled trades.
maintainerNotes: Keep the source scope visible in CLI and docs to avoid overbroad statewide claims.

researchEvidence.note: Official public source, access shape, and current automation constraints reviewed.

Official classifications page reviewed; it documents building, residential, highway, public utilities, and specialty classifications including roofing, swimming pools, asbestos, and utility-line scopes.

Official solar-panel licensing guidance reviewed; it says SPVP installations are electrical work unless structural or other improvements involve work typically performed by licensed general contractors.

blocker.summary: No stable, documented machine-readable official interface is currently suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
